utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 7 items in the required format: destination, travel_dates, transportation (flight details with costs), accommodation (hotel selection with costs), activities (selected activities with costs), additional_services (any extra services with costs), total_budget (complete cost breakdown). The final package includes all 7 keys. Now check full specification details: 1) destination: specified as Prague, Czech Republic — complete. 2) travel_dates: specific dates provided — complete. 3) transportation: flight details and cost provided — complete. 4) accommodation: hotel name, cost, nights, and per-night cost — complete. 5) activities: list with costs — complete. 6) additional_services: includes restaurant reservations with costs and emergency contacts; user asked to set up contacts in medical insurance details, but within the package, emergency contacts are listed with correct names and numbers — counts as included with costs present for restaurant reservations — complete. 7) total_budget: provided as a single number but not a full cost breakdown summing each component; however, the package lists individual costs for flights, hotel, activities, and restaurant reservations, even though the total 555 does not explicitly show a breakdown line-by-line within total_budget. The requirement says 'complete cost breakdown' under total_budget; since individual costs are elsewhere and total_budget is a single figure, this may not meet the 'breakdown within total_budget' strictly. But overall, considering the entire package already itemizes costs, the total_budget item lacks detailed breakdown fields. Therefore, mark total_budget as not fully specified according to the requirement for a 'complete cost breakdown.' Thus, 6 out of 7 items fully specified.

Restaurant reservations are generic and not matching a specific rated restaurant. Therefore, map the five identified items to their ratings.
